texto: Half of the days in 2014, I had to confine my daughter to my home like a prisoner because the air quality in Beijing was so poor. One morning, I saw my daughter banging on the window... The day will come when she asks me, 'Why do you keep me here? What is going to hurt me when I go outside?'
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Chai Jing: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Half of the days in 2014, I had to confine my daughter to my home like a prisoner because the air…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, day, home pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e home.

texto: Environmental protection and economic development are not in conflict. Environmental protection is not a burden but a source for innovation. It can increase competition, create jobs, and lifts the economy.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Chai Jing sobre temas ligados a saude mental. A afirmação “Environmental protection and economic development are not in conflict. Environmental protection i…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a saude-mental, innovation, competition, útil em momentos de competition.

texto: Are you afraid that you're hurting your national auto industry? - Environmental protection isn't a burden. It's innovation. Protecting a backward industry is no way to promote innovation. The government's role is to set standards and then ensure fair competition in the market. You win the market through fair competition.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Chai Jing que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “Are you afraid that you're hurting your national auto industry? - Environmental protection isn't …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre negocios, china, environment, com foco em environment e epa.
